definition:
Minecraft iteratively runs its processing code in a loop. One cycle of this loop is called a Tick or Gametick (gt). 
Gameticks are also used as the default measurement of time while working with redstone. The game attempts to run 20 gameticks a second (20tps), but will fail to do so and slow down the game if the server is lagging (>50mspt). 
Often confused with the less technical term "redstone tick". A redstone tick is in practice 2gt, as its origin comes from the default setting of a repeater. 
A default repeater when freshly placed is a 2gt- or one redstone-tick-repeater.
